desc: Amendment to BCO 15-1-14 on Theological Examining Committee composition and procedures, passed down to presbyteries for advice and consent
Amendment to BCO 14-1.14 expanding scope of TEC to examine all first and second level administrative officers, adopted and sent to presbyteries for advice and consent
BCO Preface adopted by First General Assembly
BCO Chapters I-X adopted
BCO Chapters XI-XX adopted
BCO Chapters XX-XVII adopted
Part II, The Rules of Discipline, adopted
Part III, The Directory for the Worship of God, adopted
Directory for Worship adopted; suspension of BCO 26-2 ended; Constitution declared fully adopted
BCO 23-1 amendment re co-pastors sent down to presbyteries (defeated in 1989)
Proposed amendment to BCO Chapter 15 regarding judicial procedures (Standing Judicial Commission), approved and sent to presbyteries for action
Change 'twenty' to 'nineteen' and delete Investor's Fund for Building and Development reference, sent through normal BCO amendment process
Added sentence clarifying time limitations for TE without a call who relocates to a new presbytery
Removed emergency requirement for calling a special meeting of presbytery (Potomac overture)
Raised requirement for calling special meeting of presbytery, allowing presbytery to set own requirements
Amended BCO 13-1 regarding RE representation at presbytery meetings
Added sentence clarifying time limitations for TE without a call who relocates
Removed emergency requirement for calling a special meeting of presbytery
Raised requirement for calling special meeting of presbytery with presbytery-set minimums
Added explicit words against women expounding and preaching in public worship
Added function of Session to ensure Word preached only by qualified men
Session to ensure Word of God preached only by sufficiently qualified men
Allow latitude in oversight of mission churches, options not mutually exclusive
Extensive changes to BCO 5 for clarity, chronological order, and consistency
Added sentence specifying assistants to deacons are not officers of the church
Extensive changes to BCO 5 for clarity, chronological order, and consistency
Assistants to deacons are not officers of the church and not subjects for ordination
New paragraph clarifying ability of presbyteries to assist churches with insufficient elders
Clarified how mission churches can be formed
Separated commissioning acts granting evangelist powers to receive/dismiss members vs. examine/ordain/install REs and Deacons
Clarified how mission churches can be formed
Separated commissioning acts of Presbytery for evangelists
Added explicit requirement for reporting and reviewing minutes of a Mission Church temporary system of government
Added explicit requirement for reporting and reviewing minutes of a Mission Church temporary government
Changed qualifications language for Elders, replacing 'grave' with 'spiritually fruitful, dignified'
Added words 'and demonstrate hospitality' to Elder qualifications
Assembly approved amendment to BCO 13-1
Ordained ministers from other denominations must declare differences with Westminster Standards for presbytery judgment
Presbyteries voted in favor; Assembly approved amendment to BCO 13-6 on ordained ministers from other denominations
Added requirement for annual reports from ministers without call
Presbyteries voted in favor; Assembly approved amendment to BCO 13-2 on annual reports from ministers without call
Increase Permanent Program Committee membership from 14 to 15 men, extending terms from four to five years
Presbyteries ratified amendment increasing Permanent Program Committee membership to 15 and terms to five years
Amend BCO 14-1 to allow implementation of new AC funding model
Amend BCO 14-2 to allow implementation of new AC funding model
Adding Reformed University Ministries to the BCO as a Permanent Committee
Amendments adding Reformed University Ministries ratified after presbyteries approved 53-1
Name change of Insurance, Annuities and Relief to PCA Retirement and Benefits, Inc.
Presbyteries ratified amendment recognizing name change from Insurance, Annuities and Relief to PCA Retirement and Benefits, Inc.
Clarify difference between a presbytery commission appointed as an interim session and other presbytery commissions
Define quorum for a presbytery-appointed commission as one more than half its membership
Define quorum for a presbytery-appointed commission as one more than half its membership (presbyteries voted 72-1)
Amend BCO 15-4 to clarify all matters of discipline (not just judicial cases) are referred to the SJC
Alter BCO 40-5 to remove reference to judicial memorials and simplify language
Presbyteries voted 49-16 to amend BCO 15-4; Assembly approved the amendment
Presbyteries voted 54-11 to amend BCO 40-5; Assembly approved the amendment
Add sentence to end of BCO 15-4 instituting term limitations for SJC members
Amend BCO 18-7 to require recording sufficient reason for removal or withdrawal of a candidate from care of Presbytery
Presbyteries voted 64-1 in favor; Assembly approved amendment to BCO 18-7
Amend BCO 19-2 to require licentiates to state differences with Westminster Standards and require presbyteries to rule on their nature
Presbyteries voted 67-2 in favor; Assembly adopted amendment to BCO 19-2
Amend BCO 19-11 to specify examination requirements for an intern transferring internship between presbyteries
Presbyteries approved amendment 63-0; Assembly adopted amendment to BCO 19-11
Added concluding sentence describing role of pulpit committee in calling a pastor
Added two explanatory paragraphs clarifying subscription standards and presbytery's right to determine allowable differences
Amendment adding two explanatory paragraphs on subscription finally adopted after presbyteries voted 45-19
Added words 'and unity' to the sixth ordination question after 'purity and peace'
Amendment adding 'and unity' to sixth ordination question finally adopted after presbyteries voted 58-6
Substituted sentences clarifying procedure for electing persons to offices of RE and Deacon after presbyteries voted 40-13
Added new item 'b. knowledge of Bible content' to ordination requirements for elder and deacon
Amendment adding 'knowledge of Bible content' ordination requirement finally adopted after presbyteries voted 64-1
Corrected numbering mistake in BCO 24-3
Added new BCO 24-5 giving explicit direction regarding highly divided votes for elders and deacons
Amendments to BCO 24-3 and 24-5 on highly divided votes adopted after presbyteries voted 55-8
Broadened allowable circumstances for emeritus status from age 70 to 'by reason of age or infirmity'
Amendment broadening emeritus status circumstances adopted after presbyteries voted 63-0
Allowed RE to moderate a congregational meeting of a church not his own
Allowed RE to moderate a congregational meeting of a church not his own
Allowed RE to moderate a congregational meeting of a church not his own
Amendments allowing RE to moderate congregational meeting of another church adopted after presbyteries voted 69-0
Amendments allowing RE to moderate congregational meeting of another church adopted after presbyteries voted 69-0
Amendments allowing RE to moderate congregational meeting of another church adopted after presbyteries voted 69-0
Specified quorum of one-half of resident communing members for congregational meeting voting to withdraw from PCA
Adding a separate arraignment hearing to judicial process between indictment meeting and trial
Adding a separate arraignment hearing to judicial process; presbyteries voted 48-3 and Assembly approved
Adding instructions regarding delivery and compliance requirements for indictments and citations
Adding delivery and compliance instructions for indictments and citations; presbyteries voted 48-3 and Assembly approved
Amendments specifying contumacy includes refusal to cooperate with lawful proceedings and court must act immediately; presbyteries approved 47-4
Amendments specifying contumacy includes refusal to cooperate with lawful proceedings and uniformizing language
Amendments specifying contumacy includes refusal to cooperate with lawful proceedings and uniformizing language
Amendments specifying contumacy includes refusal to cooperate with lawful proceedings and uniformizing language
Changing threshold for Assembly to assume original jurisdiction from two other presbyteries to at least ten percent of all presbyteries
Treating the censure of indefinite suspension and excommunication as separate censures
Giving presbyteries more control in determining whether a minister is ready to be restored to office
Giving presbyteries more control in determining whether a minister is ready to be restored to office
Allowing counsel by any communing member of the presbytery for judicial process before a Session, and any PCA member for process before Presbytery or SJC
Removal of deposition requires three-fourths vote of the court inflicting censure or court delegated that authority
Removal of deposition requires three-fourths vote of the court inflicting censure or court delegated that authority
Presbyteries voted 67-3 to amend BCO 34-8 on threshold for removing censure of deposition
Presbyteries voted 67-3 to amend BCO 37-6 on threshold for removing censure of deposition
Require recording of all testimony by transcription or electronic means with provision for costs
Require recording of all testimony by transcription or electronic means with provision for costs
Presbyteries voted 46-5 to require recording of testimony in trials
Presbyteries voted 46-5 to require recording of testimony in trials
Clarify directions for those under censure who move a significant distance from the court that imposed censure
Presbyteries voted 69-4 to amend BCO 37-7 on censure and relocation
Clarify when confession of guilt is a case without process, how to proceed, and right to complain
Presbyteries voted 46-4 to amend BCO 38-1 on procedure in cases without process
Clarify terminology and allow complaint after appeal is ruled out of order, withdrawn, or abandoned
Presbyteries voted 48-3 to amend BCO 43-1 on when a complaint is in order
Clarify timing of review of complaints by higher courts in judicial cases
Increase filing period for complaint to original court from thirty to sixty days
Presbyteries voted 58-11 to amend BCO 43-2 on filing period for complaint to original court
Change start date of thirty-day filing period for complaint to next higher court
Change start date of thirty-day filing period for appeal to next higher court
Presbyteries voted 67-2 to amend BCO 42-4 on filing period for appeals to next higher court
Presbyteries voted 66-3 to amend BCO 43-3 on filing period for complaints to next higher court
Clarify process for adjudicating complaints in higher court and equitable scheduling of hearings
Clarify process for adjudicating complaints in higher court and equitable scheduling of hearings
Clarify process for adjudicating complaints in higher court and equitable scheduling of hearings
Presbyteries voted 62-3 to amend BCO 43 on procedures for complaints
Add words clarifying complaint heard before higher court as opposed to lower court's own consideration
Presbyteries voted 49-1 to amend BCO 43-5 on lower court defending itself against complaint
Require higher court to accept Reference from lower court when sustaining complaint against non-indictment in doctrinal case or public scandal
Presbyteries voted 65-5 to amend BCO 43-10 on references in cases where complaints for non-indictment have been sustained
Amended BCO 58-5 to remove the word 'broken' from the communion liturgy and add a parenthetical permitting alternate biblical accounts of institution
Presbyteries voted 50-0 in favor; Assembly approved the amendment to BCO 58-5 removing 'broken' and adding parenthetical
Amended BCO 57-5 to encourage use of membership questions as printed and add introductory clause allowing alternate questions that communicate their substance
Amended BCO 59-1 and 59-6 to specify that laws regulating marriage be obeyed insofar as they do not transgress the laws of God
Minority Report adopted adding to BCO 58-5 that communicants are to eat the bread and drink the cup in separate sacramental actions
Approved submission of BCO 59 to presbyteries to be granted full constitutional authority pursuant to BCO 26-2
Amended BCO 59 to add that ministers shall only solemnize marriages between one man and one woman
